items.headline: Mongolia confirms third prime minister within a year amid economic alarm
items.body: Parliament approved former hip-hop artist Uchral Nyam-Osor to break legislative deadlock and calm fears over fuel dependence and commodity volatility. Rapid turnover reflects deepening factional struggles in ruling party as landlocked nation faces compounding external pressures from energy costs and mining revenue swings.

items.headline: Japan deploys first domestically-developed long-range missiles to southwest bases
items.body: Type-12 missiles with ranges exceeding earlier versions became operational at Camp Kengun in response to China's military expansion. Deployment marks shift from postwar defensive posture to counterstrike capability as Tokyo fortifies southwestern islands near Taiwan and disputed waters.

items.headline: Britain cuts Africa aid by more than half to fund rearmament
items.body: Foreign Office redirects support away from Sierra Leone, Malawi and poorest nations toward Ukraine and Middle East conflict zones. Health officials warn withdrawn funding for disease outbreaks, maternal care and child nutrition will have generational consequences across sub-Saharan Africa.

items.headline: Rohingya refugees face sharply reduced food assistance as multiple donors retreat
items.body: World Food Program implements tiered system dropping some aid to seven dollars monthly for hundreds of thousands in Bangladesh camps. Cuts compound after major donor reductions leave overcrowded settlements with diminishing resources amid ongoing displacement crisis.
